definitions: Code that doesn't wait — runs in the background while other code continues
The device/program that makes requests to a server — the 'thing' that's asking for services
Frontend is the code/interface that runs on the client that the user sees and interacts with

types.name: Examples of clients
types.description: Your browser (Chrome, Firefox), your extension (the program running in browser), a mobile app, or even another server

types.name: Examples of frontend
types.description: HTML/CSS/JS files, UI/design, buttons, forms, visuals

note: Client = hardware/program (the thing making requests). Frontend = the code running on it (what the user sees).

definitions: Document Object Model — the webpage structure that JavaScript can read and manipulate
Code that waits for a specific action (like a click or typing) and runs a function when it happens
A browser API used to get data from the internet (make HTTP requests from JavaScript)
